rulings.Case Title: TYRONE L. ADAMS VS. CHARLES L. EASLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hrg 9/30/20 Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default Entered As To Defendant Gary Holbrook And Motion To Dismiss Gary Holbrook
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2020 line 1. DEFENDANT GARY HOLBROOK Hrg 9/30/20 Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default Entered As To Defendant Gary Holbrook And Motion To Dismiss Gary Holbrook Motion to Set Aside Default is moot. Defendant's default was entered on June 12, 2020. On June 26, 2020 Plaintiff filed a First Amended Complaint naming the defendant. Motion to Dismiss for failure to timely served and bring the case to trial is GRANTED. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DORGAN VS. EDWARD CHUTE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2020 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT KEVIN DORGAN MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is DENIED in part; GRANTED in part; and CONTINUED in part. Motion is denied as to paragraph 27 of the Cross-Complaint. This part of the motion is untimely as to the Cross-Complaint filed on August 16, 2018. Motion is CONTINUED to October 26, 2020 as to the Supplemental Cross-Complaint for additional supplemental briefing. It appears that the opposing party misunderstood the Court's instructions for the initial round of supplemental briefing. As an initial matter, the Court notes that neither the Cross-Complaint nor the Supplemental Cross-Complaint assert an independent cause of action for slander of title. Allegations regarding allegedly improper recordation of the lis pendens form factual basis of the six identified claims in the Cross-Complaint and seek relief in accordance with those claims. Therefore, upon shifting of the burden by the moving party, Cross-Complaint had to show probability of prevailing on the merits on the six claims to the extent they arise out of the recordation of the lis pendens. Hence, the Court required supplemental briefing expecting it to address the second prong of ANTI-SLAPP, i.e. to provide argument and evidence as to the probability of prevailing on the merits for the asserted claims of Intentional Interference with Contractual Relations, Elder Abuse, IIED, Fraud, Conspiracy and Constructive Trust to the extent these claims arise out of the recordation of the lis pendens as asserted in the Supplemental Cross-Complaint. While supplemental brief fully addressed not specifically asserted claim for slander of title, it failed to provide evidence to support each cause of action for all other claims. ***ADDITIONAL TEXT OF TENTATIVE RULING*** =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DORGAN VS. EDWARD CHUTE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2020 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT KEVIN DORGAN MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT ***CONTINUATION OF TENTATIVE RULING*** Cross-Defendant'S Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint Recordation of the lis pendens falls squarely within the protections of ANTI-SLAPP. La Jolla Group II v. Bruce (2012) 211 Cal. App. 4th 461. The only exception to such protection relied upon by the Cross-Complaint is stated in Salma v. Capon (2008) 161 Cal. App. 4th 1275. Cross-Complainant failed to cite any authority or provide any analysis for the proposition that recordation of a non-code compliant lis pendens is "illegal as a matter of law." No cases are cited for this proposition. Moving party has successfully shifted the burden under the first prong of ANTI-SLAPP. As to the second prong, Cross-Complainant correctly argues that recordation of a non-code complaint lis pendens in not privileged under CC 47(b). Palmer v. Zaklama (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 1367, 1380. Cross-Complaints burden at this point was to show probability of prevailing on its six claims based on the recordation of lis pendens. As stated above, Cross-Complainant seems to have misunderstood the Court's request on this point. Therefore, Cross-Complainant shall have final opportunity to address the issues as described above. Further supplemental brief (and corresponding evidence) showing probability of prevailing on the merits on the claims for Intentional Interference with Contractual Relations, Elder Abuse, IIED, Fraud, and Conspiracy shall be filed no later than October 9, 2020. Moving party may file further supplemental reply no later than October 19, 2020. Motion is GRANTED as to the cause of action for Constructive Trust of the Supplemental Corss-Complaint. Cross-Complainant agreed that this claim (arising out of recordation of lis pendens) should be withdrawn. In short, the Court finds that the moving party shifted its burden re: Supplemental Cross-Complaint. Plaintiff has shown probability of prevailing on the merits on slander of title claim; Plaintiff failed to show probability of prevailing on the merits as to the Constructive Trust claim; Plaintiff is required to file a supplemental brief (and evidence) to address probability of prevailing on the merits on the remaining claims asserted in the Supplemental Cross-Complaint. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2020 line 3. DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Plaintiff to prepare order in accordance with CCP 437c(g). =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE ET AL VS. CITIBANK , N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2020 line 4. Demurrer To Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action in good faith. Plaintiff shall also allege facts in good faith to support standing. Authorities cited by Defendant are well-taken and should be considered by Plaintiff for the amendment. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE ET AL VS. CITIBANK , N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Of Citibank, N.A.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2020 line 4. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Of Citibank, N.A. Hearing Required. By no later than 9:00 a.m. Plaintiff to provide a supplemental brief, (preferable in a table format) identifying discovery, if any, necessary to amend the complaint per Court's ruling on the demurrer. (e.g. Special Interrogatory 33, "text of the interrogatory," facts to support contract formation re: Breach of Contract cause of action). =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: AVALON OCEAN AVENUE LP VS. KEVIN LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2020 line 6. DEFENDANT KEVIN LEE MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. Complaint is properly verified and no authority is cited for the proposition that failure by the Plaintiff to verify the complaint divests the Court of personal jurisdiction. The Court notes that this motion was set for hearing in violation of CCP 1167.4. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ALTIUS ENTERPRISES LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. JUAN GILBERTO ESCOBAR MARTINEZ ET AL
